extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR Tadej won the 27km ITT beating Stefan KÜNG by 18.2 seconds but VANDERPOEL Mathieu retained yellow by 8 seconds despite never riding a TT of this magnitude.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR flying at all time checks on flat TT despite only 3rd at nationals suggests strategic peaking schedule.
VANDERPOEL limited time loss to 39sec over 27km in debut long TT reveals GC threat beyond expected duration.
Rain timing disadvantaged earlier starters like BISSEGGER who crashed, making start order critical on marginal weather days.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: dominated flat TT unexpectedly after mediocre nationals, moved to 8sec off yellow, establishing GC dominance early
rider_signals.VANDERPOEL Mathieu: positive: defended yellow by limiting losses to 39sec in first-ever long TT, proving GC legs beyond one-week races
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: beat time trial field except Pogacar by 18sec on home roads for team owners
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: rode competitively but lacked top-end speed, finished behind Küng but ahead of GC rivals
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: minimal ITT losses post-crash suggests recovery capacity, stayed in top 10 GC
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: negative: rain-affected performance 21sec worse than Bjerg despite being favorite, brilliant save but couldn't maintain speed
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: uncharacteristically poor TT performance, lost over a minute and visibly frustrated
